---
title: Deli Overview
description: A local-first developer control plane in the terminal.
---

# Deli Overview

`deli` is the terminal console for end-to-end control-plane work.

<Info>
Use deli when you want docs, config, code, and monitoring in one place.
</Info>

## Core workflow

- Read system and runbook documentation
- Inspect structured runtime configuration
- Jump into the relevant worktree or command
- Review metrics before and after an operation

## Views

### Documents

Browse Mintlify-style docs and markdown without leaving the terminal.

### Configs

Inspect provider-backed configuration as typed tabular data.

### Worktree

Use command launchers and workspace shortcuts as operational jump points.

### Monitoring

Review time-series charts with terminal-native fallbacks.

---
title: Deploy Runbook
sidebarTitle: Deploy
---

# Deploy Runbook

## Preconditions

- Working tree is clean
- CI is green
- Release branch is selected

<Steps>
### 1. Validate configuration

Confirm service image tags, replica counts, rollout strategy, and feature flags.

### 2. Stage the deployment

Apply the release in the staging target and confirm monitoring looks healthy.

### 3. Promote to production

Use the production rollout command and watch error rate, latency, and saturation.
</Steps>

## Rollback

> Roll back immediately if latency spikes or readiness drops below target.

```bash
kubectl rollout undo deploy/api -n prod
```

# Observability Checklist

Use this checklist before and after production-facing changes.

- CPU and memory are within expected bounds
- Error rate is flat or improved
- Queue depth is stable
- Background workers drain normally

## Dashboards

- `api-latency`
- `worker-throughput`
- `rollout-health`

## Notes

If the terminal does not support kitty graphics, deli falls back to text summaries for chart panes.
